status: ATTACK COMPLETE / VERIFICATION NOT RUN (session limit); findings are UNVERIFIED claims

findings.title: Global-span linear slope has ~zero power against the transient price shape this program actually observed; the prereg's power-near-1 guarantee does not hold and the bounded null is reachable unearned
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py dose_response (lines 236-252) fits np.polyfit(ranks, deltas, 1) over ALL matched pairs, ranks 2-24. PREREG lines 219-227 claim 'a true price at the minimum of interest is detected with power near 1' and the closing line claims 'if the price is real and at least the only price ever measured, this design hears it'. But the only price ever measured was TRANSIENT: RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md shows the read crossing at r=8 with recovery by rank 10-14, and knowledge dipping only 'at ranks eight and twelve' (line 56) before recovering. Recompute: a paired price of 0.0909 concentrated at rank 8 that recovers, on the realized grid (ranks 2,4,...,24,24, mean 13.85, SS 683.6), yields expected OLS slope = (8-13.85)*0.0909/683.6 = -0.00078 -- NEGATIVE and 20x below the 0.0152 bar. The power arithmetic (3.6/8.0 SEs) holds only for a price that accrues LINEARLY and is SUSTAINED to rank 24; under the observed dip-and-recover shape the design lands in NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE with near certainty even when a real price >= the minimum of interest was paid at the crossing. The verdict string ('no paired price above MDE') would then be false in its natural reading: a paired price above the disclosed bound existed and the statistic was shape-blind to it.
findings.fix: Pre-commit one of: (i) restrict the slope fit to the pre-committed span rank 2-8 (the same span the bar conversion uses; the recovery region becomes reported-not-fitted), or (ii) add a second pre-registered statistic matched to the transient shape -- max (or mean) paired delta over a pre-committed crossing window (ranks 6-10), bar 0.0909, with its own within-seed null -- and require the bounded null to clear BOTH. Delete or scope the 'power near 1' and 'this design hears it' sentences to the sustained-linear case.

findings.title: Within-seed permutation exchangeability claim is false for serially autocorrelated trajectory deltas; perm_p is anti-conservative and the disclosed MDE (perm_p95) is understated; the step-0 structural-zero pair makes it worse
findings.evidence: PREREG lines 85-91: 'Exchangeability holds by construction: under the null ... a seed's paired deltas at its own matched steps are exchangeable across its own rank labels.' Not true: within a seed the deltas are successive checkpoints of ONE LoRA optimizer trajectory per arm (train_accumulating, audit every 25 steps), so adjacent deltas share optimizer state and are serially correlated -- exchangeability requires permutation-invariance of the joint distribution, which autocorrelation breaks. slope_permutation_null (admissibility.py:541-586) shuffles rank labels within seed, DESTROYING the correlation; autocorrelated noise regressed on the monotone rank sequence produces systematically larger |slope| than the shuffled null (random-walk-vs-trend classic), so realized type-I error > alpha and perm_p95 (reported as mde_slope_approx, harness line 257) understates the true null spread -- the bounded null's disclosed bound is too tight, and the COUPLED/PARTIAL significance leg fires under the null at > 0.05. The attempt-2 receipt corroborates large trajectory excursions at constant dose (RESULT_VOID: fixed-arm battery sd 0.1111, excursions to 0.5417 'at ranks where nothing was removed'). Additionally _pairs (harness line 231-233) includes step 0, where both arms are the identical base model -- a structurally zero-variance delta at the lowest rank, which is not exchangeable with step>0 deltas and anchors the fit (open question (c): it converts any constant arm-level offset into positive slope, though recompute shows offset*0.017, sub-bar on its own -- the magnitude bar contains that mechanism but the p contamination remains). The dry suite cannot catch any of this: its synthetic jitter cancels exactly in the deltas, so the null behavior of the permutation test is never exercised.
findings.fix: Replace within-seed full permutation with within-seed CIRCULAR ROTATION of the delta series relative to the rank sequence (preserves serial structure; 12 rotations x 5 seeds is ample), or move the permutation to the seed level (sign-flip on per-seed slopes -- note 5 seeds gives min two-sided p = 1/16, so rotations are the workable choice). Exclude the step-0 structural pair from both the fit and the null (answer to (c): exclude). Report lag-1 autocorrelation of within-seed deltas in the receipt so the null's validity is itself a receipt.

findings.title: MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE transfers a T/F-channel price (0.0909 knowledge_drop) onto the generation-battery delta scale with no channel-gain calibration
findings.evidence: PREREG lines 160-174: the 0.0909 numerator is the dose run's knowledge_drop -- a T/F token-margin accuracy delta (RESULT_dose table, seed 0). The gated statistic is the GEN battery selected aggregate (greedy decode + containment), a different instrument on a different scale. The prereg itself concedes the gate establishes only ordinal not-deafness ('Not-deaf is NOT the same claim as sensitive-to-the-actual-price ... at the 0.0909 scale', lines 66-68), yet the bar assumes gain exactly 1 between channels. If the gen battery attenuates true capability loss (gain < 1), a real price at the interest scale sits under the bar (unearned bounded-null direction); if it amplifies (gain > 1), sub-interest prices clear it (unearned COUPLED direction). Neither computation in the design measures the gain.
findings.fix: Pre-commit a gain receipt: on marker-fired posctl checkpoints (data already collected), regress gen-aggregate drop on knowledge drop and report the coefficient alongside the gate; alternatively restate the bar in the gen channel's own units (e.g. k x paired-delta SE = k x 0.1021) with that provenance in the prereg. Either way the bar's units must match the statistic's units before freeze.

findings.title: Span pre-commitment (divide by 6) is labeled 'conservative' but is anti-conservative toward COUPLED, the program's favourable verdict
findings.evidence: PREREG lines 172-174: 'Choosing the wider span (divide by 6, not by 4) sets the LOWER bar -- conservative in the direction of HEARING a price.' COUPLED is this program's favourable verdict (attempt 2's refused verdict; the prereg's own 'SURPRISE' branch that restarts the paper). A lower bar makes the favourable verdict easier. Concretely: crossings at rank 6 were observed on 2 of 5 attempt-2 seeds (prereg line 170), and a sustained price of 0.0909 x (4/6) = 0.061 -- BELOW the stated minimum price of interest -- manifesting over a rank-6 crossing produces slope 0.0152 and clears the bar.
findings.fix: Either divide by the smaller observed crossing span (4), setting the bar at 0.0227 (conservative against the favourable verdict), or keep 6 but correct the direction label in the prereg and note explicitly that sub-0.0909 prices at rank-6 crossings can clear the bar.

findings.title: Rounding MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE up (0.015150 -> 0.0152) excludes the exact minimum price of interest under >=
findings.evidence: PREREG line 174 / harness line 100: 0.0909/(8-2) = 0.015150 exactly; the bar is set at 0.0152 and compute_verdict tests pooled_slope >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (line 283). A pooled slope of exactly the converted minimum price fails its own bar. Tiny, and in the direction against the favourable verdict, but the prereg's stated intent ('a price smaller than the only observed price is below this program's resolution') implies the observed price itself should clear.
findings.fix: Set MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.01515 (or 0.0151), matching the stated conversion.

findings.title: Realized pair grid is 13 pairs with a duplicated top rank, not the prereg's 12 unique ranks; SE multiples restated
findings.evidence: STEPS=300, REFIT_EVERY=25 produce audits at step 0 (rank 2), steps 25..275 (ranks 4..24), plus audit(299) (rank 24 again, harness line 408) -- 13 matched pairs with rank 24 twice. PREREG lines 222-224 model '12 matched pairs on the dose grid (ranks 2 to 24, spacing 2)', SS=572, SE(slope)=0.0043 (claimed multiples 'roughly 3.6 per-seed / 8.0 pooled'; on the stated grid these recompute to 3.56/7.96 -- fine). On the REALIZED grid SS=683.6, SE=0.0039, multiples 3.9/8.7 -- direction safe, but the duplicated rank-24 point double-weights the recovery region in the fit (compounding the shape mismatch in finding 1) and the step-0 point is the structural zero from finding 2.
findings.fix: State the realized 13-point grid (with the duplicate) in the prereg's power section; resolve step-0 and the step-299 duplicate explicitly (exclude step-0 per finding 2; either drop the step-299 audit from the fit or disclose the double weight).

findings.title: Gate power arithmetic uses one-tailed z=1.645 while the actual gate p is two-sided (Bonferroni-doubled)
findings.evidence: PREREG lines 205-213 compute MDE = (1.645 + 0.8416) x SE0 = 0.170 (36/36) and 0.320 (6/36). But _sensitivity_p (admissibility.py:138-153) doubles the min of two one-tailed p's, i.e. the effective one-sided level is alpha/2, z = 1.96. Corrected MDEs: (1.96+0.8416) x 0.0685 = 0.192 and x 0.1288 = 0.361. The prereg's qualitative conclusions survive (floor 0.70 still above resolution at full n; marginal instruments still likely to FAIL -- the safe direction), but the stated numbers are wrong.
findings.fix: Recompute the two MDE numbers with z_{1-alpha/2} = 1.96 (0.192 and 0.361) before freeze.

findings.title: mde_slope_approx is a 5% critical value, not a minimum detectable effect; the bounded-null wording overstates the exclusion
findings.evidence: Harness line 257 reports pooled['perm_p95'] as 'smallest |slope| distinguishable from this null'; PREREG line 99-100 calls it 'the minimum detectable slope'. The null 95th percentile is the significance CUTOFF -- an effect exactly at it is detected with ~50% power, not 80%. So 'NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE' can issue while true slopes modestly above the disclosed value are missed half the time. (Compounds with finding 2, which makes perm_p95 itself understated.)
findings.fix: Report both: the critical value (rename the field, e.g. crit_slope_p95) and an 80%-power MDE (scale by (z_.95+z_.80)/z_.95 ~ 1.51 under the normal approximation, or simulate); word the bounded-null verdict against the power-adjusted number.

findings.title: Open question (b): the arm-endogenous destruction marker is acceptable for a NOT-DEAF gate, but the gate ledger must disclose guard-fire rates on marked positives to exclude a fluency-collapse pass
findings.evidence: The marker (knowledge <= 0.60) is measured on a channel the gen battery never reads (independence holds) and the scope strings (NOT_DEAF_SCOPE, harness 114-117; no DECOUPLED/held strings, dry-asserted) are strict enough that no break claim can ride a pass -- the wording survives. The marker arithmetic checks out (clean 0.8182, SE sqrt(.8182x.1818/66)=0.0475, 0.60 is 4.6 SEs below clean). Residual gap: lam=0 training with pure erasure pressure plausibly degrades FLUENCY, and the repetition/echo guards score degenerate decodes 0 -- so the gate can pass because posctl produces degenerate text, certifying not-deaf-to-format-collapse rather than not-deaf-to-capability-destruction. The per-checkpoint guard-fire counts are collected (points carry repetition_guard_fires) but the admissibility_gate dict (harness 189-227) does not summarize them for the positive class, and the prereg's closing sentence 'if the price is real ... this design hears it' rides past the not-deaf scope (see finding 1).
findings.fix: Add to the gate ledger: repetition- and echo-guard fire counts/rates on the marker-fired posctl positives; pre-commit a disclosure rule (if a majority of positives score 0 via a guard, the not-deaf claim carries a 'fluency-collapse-dominated' qualifier in the result). Delete or scope the closing 'hears it' sentence.

findings.title: Sign-majority denominator excludes slope=None seeds while their deltas remain in the pooled permutation
findings.evidence: dose_response (harness 252-261): slopes drops seeds with < 2 distinct ranks (slope None), and sign_majority = n_pos > len(slopes)/2 uses that reduced denominator; but stat/dose/unit (line 251) accumulate ALL admissible seeds' deltas, so a None-slope seed still moves pooled_slope and perm_p. A seed contributing to the significance leg but not to the replication-consistency leg is a conformance divergence from 'the SEED is the replication unit' (PREREG line 92). Edge case (requires total rank stall past MIN_PAIRS_PER_SEED), but cheap to close.
findings.fix: Drop None-slope seeds from the pooled stat/dose/unit as well (they carry no within-seed dose contrast by definition), and record the exclusion in per_seed meta.

findings.title: Format-invariance gate is measured on the all-7-subtask aggregate, not the selected aggregate its 0.0722 bar was derived for
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_twosided_2026_07_16.md ('The format-invariance smoke gate'): the bar 0.0722 is 'one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at the minimum selection' — i.e. the SE of the SELECTED aggregate at 3 subtasks x 16 = 48 items (sqrt(0.25/48)=0.0722). But coupling_confirm_v4.py line 447 calls CBG.format_invariance_check(decode_fn=...) with no `selected`, and capability_battery_gen.py lines 331-332 default `selected = sorted(GEN_DISJOINT_POOL.keys())` — all 7 subtasks, 112 items, SE 0.0472. So fi_ok (coupling_confirm_v4.py line 470) compares a 112-item statistic against a 48-item bar (~1.5 SE lenient), and a format-sensitive SELECTED subtask can be diluted by 4 subtasks that never gate anything. The check also runs before selection even though run_calibrate computes `survivors` (line 468) before evaluating fi_ok (line 470), so the correct statistic is available.
findings.fix: In run_calibrate, evaluate format invariance on the frozen selection: re-score or re-aggregate the FI check with selected=survivors (format_invariance_check already accepts `selected`), keeping the 0.0722 bar tied to the statistic it was derived for; alternatively re-derive the bar in the prereg for the 7-subtask aggregate. Reconcile prereg text and code before freeze.

findings.title: Admissibility gate passes by default on any unrecognized verdict string instead of requiring verdict == ADMISSIBLE
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 219-226: `void = None` then three elifs match 'VOID_INSTRUMENT__unmeasurable' / '__insensitive' / '__nonspecific'; `gate_pass: void is None`. Any other verdict from styxx.admissibility passes the gate. Today this is closed — fire_threshold is always a float here so ADMISSIBLE_SENSITIVITY_ONLY (admissibility.py line 338) is unreachable and the only remaining verdict is ADMISSIBLE — but the freeze relies on string equality with an external module: any drift in admissibility.py's verdict vocabulary silently converts a VOID into a gate pass, i.e. an enumerate-failures-else-pass inversion of 'gate failure is a VOID, never a finding'.
findings.fix: Make the pass condition positive: gate passes iff rep.admissibility_verdict == 'ADMISSIBLE' (equivalently rep.admissible is True); map the three known VOIDs as now and hard-VOID (admissibility_unmeasurable or a crash) on anything else. One-line change plus a dry case.

findings.title: --dry duplicates two production paths instead of exercising them: the resume arms_key filter and the VOID_INSTRUMENT__unmeasurable mapping
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 746-754: the 'resume_skips_stale_arms_key' check re-implements the filter inline (its own loop over synthetic lines) rather than driving run_real's actual cache-read code (lines 532-547) — a regression in the production filter would still show 23/23. Similarly the elif at lines 220-221 (rep verdict VOID_INSTRUMENT__unmeasurable -> VOID_COUPLING__admissibility_unmeasurable, the degenerate-scores path) is never reached in dry: the 'admissibility_unmeasurable' case triggers only the count-shortfall early return at line 207, so a typo in that elif would pass dry and (given finding 2's default-pass) let a degenerate-score instrument gate a finding.
findings.fix: Factor the cache-line filter into a small function called by both run_real and the dry check; add a dry case whose gate populations have adequate counts but fully degenerate (identical) gen aggregates, asserting the verdict is VOID_COUPLING__admissibility_unmeasurable via the rep-verdict path.

findings.title: Prereg's stated-deltas list for the 'verbatim' training loop omits the lam==0 forward-skip (and the per-audit letter_token_ids recomputation)
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py train_accumulating docstring declares three deltas incl. '(3) when lam == 0 the behavioral forward is skipped entirely' (implemented at lines 395-405), and audit() line 349 recomputes CBMC.letter_token_ids(tok) per checkpoint where v3 (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 177, 212) received precomputed letter_groups. The prereg ('Arms' section) states only two deltas: 'lambda threaded as a parameter ... and the audit extended to the generation battery.' Both divergences are provably behavior-identical (0*loss contributes exactly zero gradient; letter_token_ids is deterministic in tok), but the panel's own rule is that divergence beyond the stated deltas is a finding, and the freeze document is the place the deltas must be complete.
findings.fix: Add the lam==0 forward-skip (with the mathematically-identical-loss note) and the letter_groups recomputation to the prereg's delta list before freeze. No code change needed.

findings.title: points grounding surface cannot exactly reproduce the dose deltas: rounding order differs by up to 1e-4
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py line 170: points store gen_aggregate = round(aggregate, 4), but dose_response (line 245) computes each delta via CBG.paired_delta = round(agg_fixed - agg_acc, 4) from UNROUNDED aggregates (capability_battery_gen.py line 372). An aggregate of 3 subtask scores is a multiple of (1/3)*1e-4, so round(a,4)-round(b,4) can differ from round(a-b,4) by 0.0001. The prereg names points 'the grounding surface' and the post-run sequence is 'RESULT doc certified against points' — a verifier recomputing per-seed deltas from points will hit 4th-decimal mismatches against the reported dose_response['per_seed']['deltas'].
findings.fix: Define the paired delta as the difference of the two 4dp aggregates points expose (round each arm's aggregate first, then subtract), or store gen_aggregate at higher precision in points, or state in the prereg that delta certification grounds on curves[*].battery_gen with a 1e-4 tolerance on points.

findings.title: No dose-span guard: a fully stalled accumulate arm (constant erased_rank on all admissible seeds) lands on the bounded-null verdict via NaN/singular polyfit instead of a VOID
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py dose_response: per-seed slope is None when len(set(ranks)) < 2 (line 249) and such seeds are excluded from sign-consistency, but the pooled call slope_permutation_null(stat, dose, ...) (line 252) still runs np.polyfit on the raw dose vector — if erased_rank never grows on any admissible seed the fit is singular/NaN, NaN comparisons at lines 283-287 make COUPLED and PARTIAL both False, and compute_verdict returns NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B with a meaningless MDE. The prereg anticipates only span SHRINKAGE ('If accumulation stalls below rank 24 the realized span shrinks'), not zero span; a broken run would map onto the headline null string. Low probability (prior receipts show rank reaching >= 20) but the failure lands on a reportable finding rather than a VOID.
findings.fix: Add a pre-committed dose-span guard before the pooled fit: require e.g. >= 2 distinct erased_rank values pooled (or per MIN_ADMISSIBLE_SEEDS seeds), else return VOID_COUPLING__underpowered (or a new frozen VOID) — plus a dry case for the stalled-rank curve.

findings.title: Attempt-3 recurrence: a significant sub-bar price is reachable and gets the bounded-null string, which is then false on its own receipt
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py compute_verdict (lines 283-289): COUPLED requires slope >= MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE AND p < alpha AND sign majority; PARTIAL requires slope >= bar AND p < alpha; EVERYTHING else falls to NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE__battery_not_deaf_1p5B. World: pooled_slope = 0.010, perm_p = 0.01, sign 5/5 — a real, statistically significant, dose-graded price below the 0.0152 bar. Since p < alpha implies |slope| exceeds the permutation null's 95th percentile, the receipt's own dose_response shows slope > mde_slope_approx while the frozen verdict string asserts NO paired price above the MDE. A measured price is mapped onto a no-price headline — exactly the silence-onto-claim failure that killed attempt 3. The --dry suite (8 cases, lines 673-689) has no significant-but-sub-bar fixture, so this branch is untested. Prereg lines 98-100 freeze the same logic, so this is a design defect, not a code/prereg divergence.
findings.fix: Add a frozen branch for (perm_p < STAT_ALPHA and pooled_slope < MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE), e.g. PARTIAL__price_below_min_interest_1p5B (or route it to the existing PARTIAL), so the bounded-null string is reachable only when perm_p >= STAT_ALPHA or |slope| <= mde. Add a --dry case with slope ~0.008 and p ~0.01 asserting the new branch. Update the prereg's decision paragraph and verdict list before freeze.

findings.title: mde_slope_approx is the permutation p95, labeled as a minimum detectable effect — the bounded null's bound is overstated
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py line 257: "mde_slope_approx": pooled["perm_p95"] with comment 'smallest |slope| distinguishable from this null'; prereg line 100 repeats it ('the permutation null's 95th percentile of |slope|') as 'the minimum detectable slope'. The p95 is the significance cutoff — a true slope exactly at p95 is detected with only ~50% power. The design's own precedent knows this: admissibility.py _mde_auroc (lines 122-135) adds the power term (z_{1-alpha}+z_{0.80})*SE for the AUROC MDE. The bounded null (the ANTICIPATED verdict, prereg line 271) would ship claiming 'no price above the minimum detectable slope' while prices up to ~1.5x p95 are missed with substantial probability — an erratum on the null bound if published.
findings.fix: Report a power-honest MDE (e.g. p95 scaled by (z_{1-alpha}+z_{0.80})/z_{1-alpha} under the normal approximation, or estimate the null SD from the permutation distribution and apply the same formula _mde_auroc uses), or rename the field and every prereg/verdict mention to 'detection limit (p95, ~50% power)' and state the ~80%-power MDE alongside. Fix both the harness key and prereg line 100 before freeze.

findings.title: Transfer-lesson recurrence: format-invariance is validated on the clean base only, but the instrument gates fine-tuned checkpoints — dose-correlated format degradation can masquerade as a COUPLED price
findings.evidence: Prereg lines 148-156 freeze the rule 'an instrument that moves under a format-only perturbation is measuring fluency/format, and no verdict may issue from it', but format_invariance_check runs only at --calibrate on the clean base (coupling_confirm_v4.py run_calibrate lines 467-470); audit() (lines 342-363) never measures it at checkpoints. This is the same shape as tonight's private-13 lesson: a property demonstrated on one population (clean base) assumed to transfer to the population actually gated (LoRA fine-tuned checkpoints) — and attempt 2's named disease is precisely that fine-tuning moves format wholesale. The paired contrast does NOT difference this out: the accumulate arm's erasure pressure grows with union rank while the fixed arm is pinned at rank 2, so any rank-graded instruction-following/format drift (e.g. preamble pushing the answer past the 8-token decode window while knowledge is intact) loads directly onto the dose slope and can produce an unearned COUPLED. Neither guard catches it (repetition/echo guards catch collapse, not verbose drift), and the gate only certifies not-deaf.
findings.fix: Measure format_invariance_check (or at minimum the verbose-wrapper aggregate) at a pre-committed subsample of checkpoints per arm and REPORT it; pre-commit in the prereg that a COUPLED result must disclose the checkpoint-level fi deltas and that a dose-graded fi delta comparable to the dose-graded paired delta demotes COUPLED to a named VOID or PARTIAL. Cheap alternative floor: score every audit under both wrappers and make the plain-vs-verbose paired-delta concordance a reported receipt.

findings.title: MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE transplants a knowledge-channel price (0.0909, T/F readout) onto the gen-aggregate channel with no cross-channel calibration
findings.evidence: Prereg lines 160-174: the 0.0909 minimum price of interest is the dose run's coupled seed's knowledge_drop (RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md, seed-0 table) — a T/F knowledge-channel quantity — divided by the rank span to gate a GENERATION-battery slope (coupling_confirm_v4.py line 100). Nothing shows the gen aggregate responds to the same damage with the same gain as the knowledge readout; the gate explicitly certifies not-deaf only, not scale (prereg lines 60-76). If the gen channel's gain differs from the knowledge channel's, the bar is mis-scaled in either direction (COUPLED too cheap, or a real price of interest classified sub-bar — which lands in the fatal finding's hole).
findings.fix: Own the bar in the prereg as a policy constant in gen-aggregate accuracy points per rank (both channels are item-accuracy fractions, so state that equivalence as the assumption it is), and pre-commit a reported (non-gating) channel-gain receipt: on marker-fired posctl checkpoints, the ratio of gen-aggregate drop to knowledge drop, so the bar's scale assumption is auditable after the run.

findings.title: The rank-span choice is framed as 'conservative' but it lowers the bar for the program-favourable verdict
findings.evidence: Prereg lines 172-174: 'Choosing the wider span (divide by 6, not by 4) sets the LOWER bar -- conservative in the direction of HEARING a price, not of manufacturing silence.' Hearing a price = COUPLED = the disclosed favourable/surprise verdict (line 271-275) that restarts the paper conversation. A lower MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE makes the favourable branch easier whenever p < alpha; the actual anti-favourable protections are significance, sign majority, and the gate. The framing inverts the direction the house rule (refuse favourable verdicts) cares about.
findings.fix: Reword the provenance paragraph: state plainly that the wider span lowers the COUPLED magnitude bar, that this trades a smaller false-silence risk for a larger favourable-verdict risk, and name the significance + sign-majority + gate requirements as the guards carrying the favourable-verdict side. No number change needed.

findings.title: Early-checkpoint paired deltas are deterministically ~0 (arms identical until the first refit), violating the within-seed exchangeability the permutation null rides on
findings.evidence: train_accumulating (coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 317-330, 366-379): both accumulate and fixed arms use torch.manual_seed(seed), the same np rng, the same subs0, and identical loss (lam=1.0 both) until the step-25 refit, so the step-0 pair is exactly the base model twice (zero-init LoRA B) and the step-25 pair differs only by bf16 nondeterminism. These low-dose deltas carry ~zero noise while step>0 deltas carry full fine-tuning noise; permuting rank labels within seed (dose_response, lines 236-252) treats them as exchangeable with high-noise late deltas, which they are not — heteroscedasticity by construction at the low-dose anchor, mildly miscalibrating the permutation p in either direction.
findings.fix: Either exclude the step-0 (and optionally step-25) pairs from the permutation test while keeping them as reported curve anchors, or disclose the non-exchangeability in the prereg's exchangeability paragraph (lines 85-91) with the direction-of-error argument. Pre-commit whichever before freeze; do not decide after data.

findings.title: Certifier phrasing gotcha: the 'Inherited bars' line carries eight gating numbers without leading-operator / 'threshold of X' phrasing
findings.evidence: Prereg lines 240-243: 'Survival threshold 0.70; clean read floor 0.75 ...; bite = frozen-deploy read below clean minus 0.05; selection floor 0.90 ...; clean selected aggregate floor 0.80; clean per-sub-task floor 0.70; at least 6 matched pairs per seed; at least 3 dose-admissible seeds.' — noun-first, no 'the threshold of X' / 'the floor of X' phrasing, unlike every other bar in the document (e.g. lines 41, 51-53, 96, 153). Per the styxx.certify number-grounding gotcha this is the one block at risk of failing OATH grounding when the RESULT doc cites these bars. (The ASCII sweep is otherwise clean: zero non-ASCII bytes in the prereg — no U+2212 minus, no em dashes.)
findings.fix: Rewrite the Inherited bars section with the certifier-safe pattern used everywhere else: 'the survival threshold of 0.70', 'the clean read floor of 0.75', 'the selection floor of 0.90', 'the aggregate floor of 0.80', 'the per-sub-task floor of 0.70', 'at least 6 matched pairs', 'at least 3 dose-admissible seeds', and give bite its exact form ('frozen-deploy read below clean minus the margin of 0.05').

findings.title: The 'no threshold ever touches the population it gates' sentence overstates: DERIVE_SEEDS fixed-arm records are dual-use (threshold derivation AND the dose-response statistic)
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py docstring lines 14-15 and prereg lines 188-190 claim population disjointness for the fire_threshold. True for the admissibility gate (derive = fixed seeds {0,1}, gate = seeds {2,3,4}) — but the same seed-{0,1} fixed-arm checkpoints that set the conformal threshold re-enter downstream as the pairing baselines in the pooled dose-response slope (dose_response runs over ALL admissible seeds, line 282 / lines 236-252). The threshold does not gate the slope, so this is not the private-13 failure, but the blanket sentence invites exactly the wrong reading and would be quoted against the design if the finding branch is ever audited.
findings.fix: Scope the sentence: 'the fire_threshold never touches the population whose fire rate it gates (gate seeds are disjoint from derive seeds); derive-seed fixed-arm checkpoints do re-enter the dose-response statistic, which the threshold does not gate.' One-line prereg edit plus the harness docstring.

findings.title: Within-seed rank-permutation null is not an error model for deterministic paired-delta trajectories -- pooled p is a trend detector and the bounded-null MDE is understated
findings.evidence: The prereg claims exchangeability 'holds by construction' (PREREG_B2_coupling_twosided_2026_07_16.md lines 86-91), but the construction gives the opposite: decode is greedy/deterministic (capability_battery_gen.py make_decoder, do_sample=False, line 289) and both arms share torch/np seed and batch order (coupling_confirm_v4.py line 319), so within a seed the delta curve fixed(t)-acc(t) is a smooth, essentially deterministic trajectory with NO exchangeable noise component across steps -- and rank is monotone in step. slope_permutation_null (admissibility.py lines 541-586, consumed at coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 236-261) shuffles rank labels within seed, so ANY smooth systematic arm divergence yields a near-zero p regardless of whether it is dose noise or dose signal: the test answers 'is there a trend', not 'is the trend larger than the design's real noise'. The declared replication unit -- the seed, n=5 (prereg line 92-94) -- never enters the p or the MDE. Consequences: (a) mde_slope_approx = perm_p95 (line 257) reflects within-curve jitter, not seed-level uncertainty, so the ANTICIPATED verdict NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE would ship with an unearned, too-small bound as its headline number; (b) PARTIAL__coupling_sign_split needs only pooled slope + this p (line 287-288), so it is reachable from a decorative p; (c) COUPLED is only partially guarded by the seed-level sign majority (binomial protection ~0.5 under seed-symmetric drift, not alpha=0.05). The prereg's own SE arithmetic (lines 216-227, sqrt(0.25/48) per checkpoint) assumes fresh item draws per checkpoint; the item set is fixed and the decode deterministic, so errors persist across checkpoints -- the arithmetic describes an experiment that is not this one.
findings.fix: Two additions before freeze, both zero-GPU relative to the planned run: (1) make the shipped p and MDE SEED-LEVEL -- 5 per-seed slopes as the units (e.g., sign-flip permutation or t across seeds; MDE derived from that null) -- and demote the within-seed permutation p to a reported descriptive; (2) pre-register a placebo null computable from the run's own cells: cross-seed fixed-minus-fixed deltas (fixed seed i vs fixed seed j at matched steps, all pairs) regressed on seed i's accumulate rank labels, with a pre-committed requirement that the slope rule's realized fire rate on this placebo is <= alpha before any COUPLED/PARTIAL p or any MDE number may ship. Add both to --dry.

findings.title: Gate sensitivity p and its power arithmetic treat up to 72 autocorrelated checkpoints as independent units; the seed-cluster risk is disclosed for the conformal threshold but not for the gate's own permutation test
findings.evidence: admissibility_gate feeds checkpoint-level scores to instrument_admissibility (coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 214-218), whose sensitivity p permutes labels over individual checkpoints (admissibility.py _sensitivity_p, lines 138-153). The positives are at most 3 posctl trajectories and the nulls 3 fixed trajectories; with deterministic decode, within-trajectory scores are persistent, so the effective replication is ~3-vs-3 seeds while the permutation and the prereg's AUROC power arithmetic (PREREG lines 204-214: null SE sqrt((36+36+1)/(12*36*36)) = 0.0685) assume 36-vs-36 independent units. This is anti-conservative in the PASS direction -- the one leg of the design where correlated data can manufacture a pass rather than a VOID -- and the pass is the precondition for every non-VOID verdict plus the 'battery_not_deaf' tag embedded in the bounded-null string. The prereg names seed-level exchangeability as a residual risk for the conformal fire threshold and backstops it with the realized fire-rate re-measurement (lines 196-200), but the sensitivity leg has no analogous disclosure and no analogous backstop.
findings.fix: Add a pre-committed per-seed replication requirement to the sensitivity leg: per-gate-seed AUROC (that seed's marker-fired posctl checkpoints vs that seed's fixed checkpoints) >= AUROC_FLOOR in a pre-committed number of gate seeds (all 3, or 2 of 3 -- pick before freeze), with the pooled checkpoint-level p demoted to descriptive. Disclose the trajectory-autocorrelation assumption in the prereg's gate power-arithmetic section, mirroring the conformal section's residual-risk paragraph. Cover with a dry case where one seed's posctl trajectory separates and the others do not.

findings.title: MIN_POSITIVE_MARKED=6 can be satisfied entirely by one gate seed -- gate sensitivity certifiable from a single trajectory
findings.evidence: The positive population filter (coupling_confirm_v4.py lines 194-196, threshold at line 107) requires only len(pos) >= 6 with no cross-seed spread requirement; if lambda=0 drives knowledge <= 0.60 on >= 6 checkpoints of only one gate seed, the not-deaf certification rests on one trajectory while the prereg's framing (lines 205-209, 'up to 36 marker-fired positives ... from 3 gate seeds') and the destruction-marker section (lines 234-236) read as 3-seed evidence. All other marker failure directions correctly err to VOID; this is the one path where thin evidence yields a PASS-shaped output.
findings.fix: Require marker-fired positives from at least 2 distinct gate seeds (else VOID_COUPLING__admissibility_unmeasurable); one condition in admissibility_gate, one sentence in the prereg's marker section, one new --dry case (marker fires on a single gate seed -> unmeasurable). Subsumed by the per-seed AUROC requirement of the previous finding if that fix is taken in its all-3 or 2-of-3 form.

findings.title: Echo/repetition guard zeros feed the priced statistic with no arm-differential check, and echo fires are dropped from the verdict's grounding surface
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:272-274 zeroes ANY decode containing >=2 candidates, including capable answers that contain the gold ('apple, then mango' on ALPHA_GEN, 'table, not river' on ORTH) -- with 5 of 7 disjoint sub-tasks list-format, a dose-correlated shift in decode verbosity/style (not capability) converts directly into paired price: delta = fixed_agg - accumulate_agg (coupling_confirm_v4.py:245). The fixed arm matches honesty-lambda training but NOT accumulated-erasure side effects, so erasure-induced style drift is unmatched and prices as 'capability'. No prereg'd bar ties guard-fire differentials (arm vs arm, or vs dose) to verdict validity. Aggravator: build_points (coupling_confirm_v4.py:171) and the receipt schema check (line 717) carry repetition_guard_fires but NOT echo_guard_fires, and PREREG line 279-280 lists the points tuple without echo fires -- the confound's receipt exists only in the bulky curves blob, invisible to verify_replication on points. An unearned COUPLED__dose_slope_price_measured_1p5B (the paper-restarting branch, prereg lines 272-274) is reachable through a channel this program's own attempt-2 VOID names.
findings.fix: Add echo_guard_fires (per checkpoint, and ideally per-arm totals) to build_points and the receipt schema. Prereg ONE subtractive check: recompute the dose slope with guard-zeroed items excluded from both arms' aggregates (or bar: |accumulate-arm guard-fire rate - fixed-arm rate| and its dose correlation); if the price does not survive, COUPLED/PARTIAL downgrades to the bounded null. Purely subtractive, cheap, pre-committed.

findings.title: Format-invariance gate is estimated on the wrong statistic and the wrong population, with a bar at 79% of the minimum price of interest
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:447 calls CBG.format_invariance_check with no `selected` argument (default = all 7 disjoint sub-tasks, capability_battery_gen.py:331-332) and it runs BEFORE selection (line 468), on the CLEAN BASE only -- while the verdict statistic is the SELECTED-sub-task aggregate on TRAINED checkpoints. This is the attempt-3 kill shape (gate estimated on a population other than the one the claim lives on): fine-tuning-induced format sensitivity, the named attempt-2 disease (prereg lines 154-156), is exactly the regime the clean-base check cannot see. The bar FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722 is 'one aggregate SE at minimum selection' (m=3, sqrt(0.25/48), prereg line 217-218) but the measured statistic pools 112 items (SE 0.047, so the bar is ~1.5 SE of what is actually measured), and 0.0722 is 79% of the 0.0909 minimum price of interest -- a format channel nearly as large as the effect being priced passes the gate. Single deterministic measurement, no CI, answering open question (d): as specified the bar is not meaningful protection.
findings.fix: Reorder --calibrate to select first, then run format_invariance_check(selected=survivors) so the gated statistic is the verdict's own aggregate; re-measure FI at end-state checkpoints (fixed-arm final + one accumulate final) as a reported receipt with a prereg'd subtractive rule (FI failure on trained checkpoints voids the price, never rescues it); state the bar as a fraction of the 0.0909 price of interest rather than one SE, or lower it.

findings.title: Gold 'may' is a hedging modal (and ANTONYM golds are all high-frequency adjectives): incidental containment inflates degraded checkpoints toward the ANTICIPATED bounded-null verdict
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:178 -- ('Which month of the year comes immediately after April?', 'may'). A partially degraded model that hedges ('It may be ...', 'That may depend') scores the item CORRECT by word-boundary containment; hedging is precisely the degraded-regime behavior. The 3-char floor (constraint (a), lines 15-17) screens chance substring hits but not function words. ANTONYM_GEN golds (down, old, short, full, dry, late, sad, cold) are top-frequency English words with nonzero incidental rates in 8-token fluff. Direction of harm: false ceiling on accumulate-arm checkpoints shrinks the paired delta, biasing toward NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_MDE -- which the prereg discloses as the ANTICIPATED verdict (lines 270-272), i.e. false insensitivity (attempt 3's mechanism) pushing toward the favourable-for-erasure branch. Gate-side false ceiling only voids (safe), but the finding-side bias is anti-conservative.
findings.fix: Drop or reword the April->May item (the only gold that is a bare auxiliary); add a selftest invariant that no gold/variant is in a frozen stoplist of high-frequency function words; optionally require SEQ_GEN golds to match as the first content token of the decode. Cheap item edit before freeze; nothing else changes.

findings.title: Gate sensitivity can be carried entirely by repetition-guard zeros, certifying the guard channel rather than the containment channel that prices dose
findings.evidence: score_item checks is_degenerate first (capability_battery_gen.py:267-268): gross-destroyed posctl checkpoints (knowledge <= 0.60) with collapsed decodes get gen_aggregate ~0 via the repetition guard, which alone can produce the gate's AUROC and fire-rate pass (coupling_confirm_v4.py:194-218) -- 'battery not deaf' is then demonstrated for decode-collapse detection while the dose price on the accumulate arm must be heard through containment misses at mild doses, a different mechanism of the same instrument. The prereg's NOT_DEAF scope (gross destruction only, v4:114-117) partially bounds this, which is why this is minor rather than major.
findings.fix: Report the guard-fire fraction among marker-fired gate positives in the admissibility ledger; add one sentence to the prereg scope noting that gate sensitivity may be guard-driven and does not certify containment-channel sensitivity at sub-gross doses.

findings.title: Accepted variants are never predicate-verified; ANTONYM predicate is a mirrored copy of the bank
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:432 asserts pred(gold) only; variants ('little', 'unhappy', 'shut', 'heavy', 'easy', 'silent') widen acceptance but are checked solely for the 3-char floor and echo safety (lines 430, 441-449), so the docstring's 'recomputes EVERY gold from ground-truth predicates' does not extend to the acceptance set. The ANTONYM predicate itself (_ANTONYM, lines 376-379) is a hand-written duplicate of the bank rather than an independent recomputation -- the weakest non-circularity in the selftest.
findings.fix: Make _ANTONYM set-valued (gold + variants) and assert every accepted answer satisfies it; for other sub-tasks assert variants satisfy the same predicate as the gold (currently all variant-bearing items are ANTONYM, so this is one small edit); disclose the antonym-map exemption in the prereg's selftest paragraph.

findings.title: Punctuation-split containment lets digit-grouped/decimal outputs match numeric golds ('1,200' matches '200')
findings.evidence: _norm_tokens (capability_battery_gen.py:229) maps every non-alphanumeric to a space, so '1,200' -> ['1','200'] and '200.5' -> ['200','5'], both containing gold '200' -- the documented invariant "'1200' does NOT match a gold of '200'" (lines 33-34) holds only for unpunctuated digit runs, and the selftest (line 452) tests only the '1200' case. Exposure is limited to MUL_GEN, which is bank-adjacent, selection-excluded, and REPORTED-only (lines 194-197, 525), so no verdict path is touched; the reported three-channel overlay could carry a small false ceiling.
findings.fix: Strip grouping commas/periods between digits before tokenizing (or merge adjacent all-digit tokens for matching), and add '1,200 vs 200' and '200.5 vs 200' selftest cases; alternatively document the limitation next to the invariant.
